LIMIT = 8145

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A08": 1059, "B95": 300, "A39": 514, "A65": 1057, "B81": 334, "B34": 719, "A28": 140, "C82": 729, "B69": 343, "A87": 741, "B64": 326, "B15": 346, "C79": 827, "B16": 501, "C53": 485, "C83": 312, "C10": 733, "A97": 252, "A25": 834, "C84": 403, "A71": 858, "B23": 833, "B96": 997, "C51": 465, "C17": 98, "B19": 826, "C72": 818, "A94": 800, "A46": 411, "B43": 551, "B37": 606, "A88": 271, "A96": 788, "C60": 246, "A75": 128}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

Let me rebuild without the initially included smaller pieces—removing A28, A75, and C17—then reassess whether a different core set yields better overall value.

Starting fresh with A08, A65, B96, A71, A25, B23, C79, B19, C72, A96 totals 7937 effort and 8897 importance. This leaves 208 effort to work with. B95 fits perfectly at 204 effort with 300 importance, bringing the total to 8141 and pushing importance to 9197. With just 4 effort remaining, only C17's 2-effort cost allows it to squeeze in, completing the selection.

Now testing A88 as an alternative: swapping it in instead of B95 gives 8112 effort and 9168 importance. The remaining 33 effort accommodates A75 (32 effort, 128 importance), reaching 8144 effort and 9296 importance. This configuration yields slightly higher importance than the B95 path. Exploring further by removing C72 to free up 722 effort opens different possibilities for optimization. B96, A71, A25, B23, C79, B19, A96, A87, B95, A75, A28, C17

Let me verify the totals: effort comes to 8142 and importance reaches 9486. This configuration works well.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 8145

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A08": 963, "B95": 204, "A39": 418, "A65": 961, "B81": 238, "B34": 623, "A28": 44, "C82": 633, "B69": 247, "A87": 645, "B64": 230, "B15": 250, "C79": 731, "B16": 405, "C53": 389, "C83": 216, "C10": 637, "A97": 156, "A25": 738, "C84": 307, "A71": 762, "B23": 737, "B96": 901, "C51": 369, "C17": 2, "B19": 730, "C72": 722, "A94": 704, "A46": 315, "B43": 455, "B37": 510, "A88": 175, "A96": 692, "C60": 150, "A75": 32}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A08": 1059, "B95": 300, "A39": 514, "A65": 1057, "B81": 334, "B34": 719, "A28": 140, "C82": 729, "B69": 343, "A87": 741, "B64": 326, "B15": 346, "C79": 827, "B16": 501, "C53": 485, "C83": 312, "C10": 733, "A97": 252, "A25": 834, "C84": 403, "A71": 858, "B23": 833, "B96": 997, "C51": 465, "C17": 98, "B19": 826, "C72": 818, "A94": 800, "A46": 411, "B43": 551, "B37": 606, "A88": 271, "A96": 788, "C60": 246, "A75": 128}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

You will now receive the first message of the other player.

PROPOSAL: {'C17', 'A75', 'A28', 'C60', 'A97', 'A88', 'B95', 'C83', 'B64', 'B81', 'B69', 'B15', 'C84', 'A46', 'C51', 'C53', 'B16', 'A39', 'B43', 'B37', 'A08', 'A65', 'B96'}

ARGUMENT: {'This proposal includes a balanced mix of items with total effort of 7937, which is well within our limit of 8145. I've prioritized items with good value-to-effort ratios while ensuring we maximize the use of our available capacity. This set includes 23 items that collectively provide strong coverage across all item categories (A, B, and C items).'}

That's within the 8145 limit. Now I need to evaluate how much importance this proposal delivers to me. Looking at the importance values for each item, the total comes to 10,687 - a solid outcome that addresses most of my key priorities while staying within the constraint.

The proposal captures my top-value items like A08, A65, B96, A71, A25, B23, C79, B19, A96, and A87. However, I'm missing some valuable options: C72 (818 importance), A94 (800 importance), C10 (733 importance), C82 (729 importance), and B34 (719 importance) - all of which would require significant additional effort. With only 3 units of effort remaining, I should explore whether swapping out lower-priority items might yield better overall value.

Removing B95, A75, A28, and C17 would free up 282 effort while sacrificing 666 importance. Looking at what I could add with that budget, the high-value items still exceed the available capacity - C72 needs 722 effort, A94 needs 704, C10 needs 637, C82 needs 633, and B34 needs 623. Even B37 (606 effort) and B43 (455 effort) are too demanding for the freed-up space.

I should consider a different approach: swapping lower-efficiency items for higher-value ones. The current proposal contains some weak performers - C17 has a 49 importance-to-effort ratio, A28 sits at 3.18, A75 at 4, and B95 at around 1.47. These are candidates for replacement if I can find better uses for their effort allocation.

The high-value items I'm missing (C72 and A94, both with ratios around 1.13-1.14) require too much effort to fit even if I remove multiple low performers. Removing B95, A75, A28, and C17 would free 282 effort but still isn't enough for C72's 722 effort requirement.

The current solution is actually quite solid at 10,486 importance with 3 units remaining. A minor tweak might help - dropping just C17 and A28 would open up 46 effort while sacrificing only 238 importance, giving me 49 total effort to work with. The top 10 items by importance are A08, A65, B96, A71, A25, B23, C79, B19, C72, and A94. Their proposal includes the first eight but excludes C72 and A94—a strong offer that captures most of my highest-value items.

I should explore whether I can construct a counteroffer that improves my position while remaining attractive to them. If I bundle the eight highest-importance items together, that uses 6523 effort and leaves 1622 available for additional selections.

Adding A96 (692 effort, 788 importance) and C72 (722 effort, 818 importance) brings me to 7937 total effort, leaving just 208 for more items. I can fit B95 (204 effort, 300 importance) and C17 (2 effort, 98 importance) within that remaining capacity, reaching 8143 effort total.

But wait—my current selection totals only 9295 importance, which is actually lower than the opponent's offer of 10,486. I overlooked A87 in my calculation. The opponent's proposal is genuinely strong and nearly optimal for me. Pushing back risks them withdrawing to something worse, so accepting makes more sense than attempting further negotiation.
